steps.message:
Update Todos
[x] Inspect environment and install required Python dependencies
[] Download model cache and create Flask inference service files
[ ] Start service in background and verify endpoint behavior
Planning file creation

I need to create files, but first, I should read existing ones before overwriting. If they don’t exist, I can write directly. Checking with a simple ls command should help. I'll need to use write instead of shell commands. It’s important to create the model_cache directory if it doesn’t exist, and scripts like os.makedirs can handle that. I'll write the download_model.py script first, run it, verify labels, and then write app.py. Let's get started!

Execute [/bin/sh] python3 -c "from transformers import AutoModelForSequenceClassification; model = AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained('/app/model_cache/sentiment_model'); print(model.config.id2label)"

arguments.command: python3 -c "from transformers import AutoModelForSequenceClassification; model = AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained('/app/model_cache/sentiment_model'); print(model.config.id2label)"

Execute [/bin/sh] sleep 5 && curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/sentiment -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"text": 123}'

Skill verification-specialist
Verifying files and processes

I want to do some extra verifications in parallel. I could list the required files and also run the command python -m py_compile app.py download_model.py. Then, I'm thinking about checking running processes with pgrep -af "python3 app.py"—but maybe I should use a shell command instead. Using ps -ef might be better. Also, I consider reading the app.log, so I might use a read command. Alright, let's get started on these!

results.content:
import json, urllib.request
req = urllib.request.Request('http://127.0.0.1:5000/sentiment', data=json.dumps({'text':'I love this service! It is amazing.'}).encode(), headers={'Content-Type':'application/json'})
with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as resp:
    body = json.loads(resp.read().decode())
assert body['sentiment'] == 'positive'
assert 0.0 <= body['confidence']['positive'] <= 1.0
assert 0.0 <= body['confidence']['negative'] <= 1.0
print('positive-schema-ok')
PY
positive-schema-ok

import json, urllib.request
req = urllib.request.Request('http://127.0.0.1:5000/sentiment', data=json.dumps({'text':'This is the worst experience ever.'}).encode(), headers={'Content-Type':'application/json'})
with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as resp:
    body = json.loads(resp.read().decode())
assert body['sentiment'] == 'negative'
assert 0.0 <= body['confidence']['positive'] <= 1.0
assert 0.0 <= body['confidence']['negative'] <= 1.0
print('negative-schema-ok')
PY
negative-schema-ok

import os
required = [
    '/app/model_cache/sentiment_model/config.json',
    '/app/model_cache/sentiment_model/model.safetensors',
    '/app/model_cache/sentiment_model/tokenizer_config.json',
    '/app/model_cache/sentiment_model/vocab.txt',
    '/app/app.py',
    '/app/download_model.py',
    '/app/app.log',
]
missing = [p for p in required if not os.path.exists(p)]
assert not missing, missing
print('artifacts-ok')
PY
artifacts-ok

import subprocess
out = subprocess.check_output(['ps', '-ef'], text=True)
lines = [line for line in out.splitlines() if 'python3 app.py' in line and 'sh -c' not in line]
assert lines, out
print(lines[0])

I'm also pondering whether I should reference the final statuses and perhaps the specific file references too. It feels a bit like I’m gathering all the pieces I need to figure this out! Update Todos
[x] Inspect environment and install required Python dependencies
[x] Download model cache and create Flask inference service files
[x] Start service in background and verify endpoint behaviorDone.

Files created:
• /app/download_model.py:1
• /app/app.py:1

Model cached at:
• /app/model_cache/sentiment_model

Service status:
• Running in background on 0.0.0.0:5000
• Verified process: python3 app.py

Key implementation points:
• Model is downloaded first and loaded from local disk in                       
  /app/model_cache/sentiment_model
• Flask endpoint is POST /sentiment
• Returns:
    ◦ sentiment: positive or negative
    ◦ confidence.positive
    ◦ confidence.negative
• Invalid or missing input returns HTTP 400 with {"error": ...}

Verified:
• Positive request returns positive sentiment
• Negative request returns negative sentiment
• Missing text returns 400
• Non-string text returns 400
• Python files compile successfully

Useful paths:
• Flask app: /app/app.py:1
• Download script: /app/download_model.py:1
• Log file: /app/app.log
